issue_body:
Build a creator-facing transcript review step where the user can fix speaker names, brand spellings, topic terms, and caption text before final export. Corrections should update the episode transcript, on-screen captions, title moments, and publish package copy so the finished episode feels accurate and professionally prepared.

## Acceptance (definition of done)
A reviewer can open an episode, see a transcript/caption review area populated from the episode and social context, edit speaker labels and text for key lines, apply those corrections, and observe the corrected names and wording reflected in captions, visual moments, export metadata, and publish package text. The existing verification command `node scripts/run-tests.mjs` passes with tests covering the correction flow.

---
This is the current active step for this product. The maintainer will only merge a pull request that completes THIS step and demonstrably works. Open a PR that closes this issue.

_Why this step now: The product already has the main creation, styling, editing, template, export, review, and publish-package path. The next biggest product gap is accuracy control before publishing: creators need a simple way to correct generated wording and names once, then have those fixes carry through every visible and publishable output._
